---
title: "Eliza Callahan Cleveland"
---

## Eliza Callahan Cleveland

**Dates:** (1839-1914)

### Description

American correspondent of CMY

### Biography

She is described as contributing sketches of good works among negroes to <i>Mission Life</i> 1867. She and her mother are mentioned as friends in Elizabeth Sewell\'s autobiography, which was probably how the correspondence with CMY arose.

She was the daughter of Sarah Paine Perkins and Henry Russell Cleveland (1808-1843) and was brought up in the New England political and literary establishment.  She was the niece of the art collector Edward Newton Perkins (1820-1899) and was travelling with him and her mother in Italy in 1859 when they were attacked by local revolutionaries, causing a diplomatic incident.

Eugene Hollahan \'Intertextual Bondings Between \\""The Wreck of the Hesperus\\"" and \\""The Wreck of the Deutschland\\""\' <i>Texas Studies in Literature and Language</i>  33 (1991) discusses her correspondence with Longfellow which she communicated to CMY who published it in <i>MP</i>.

There is a large collection of the Cleveland and Perkins papers in the New York Public Library.

### Letters

- Letter from April 2d 1881
- Letter from Aug 15th 1877 
- Letter from March 27th 1867
- Letter from May 10th 1865
- Letter from Jany 25th 1865

---

[← Back to all letters](../letters.html)
